title: Active Combat Skills
problem: Combat has exactly one player action: attack. No expression, no build identity beyond stats. Class-specific skills give players a mana-gated second dimension — each class has 4 unique skills unlocked by level, rewarding progression and creating distinct playstyle identities.
analysis: CombatScreen has 3 actions: Attack, Potion, Flee. No skills exist. Mana stat exists on items (increases mana pool) but gameStore has no mana tracking. Skills require: a mana pool in gameStore (persistent like HP), a skill definition table (class-tagged), level-gated unlock, and CombatScreen UI slots. The class system (feat-2026-043) determines which skill set each player sees — only their class's 4 skills are available. This replaces the original generic-skills design with a 3×4 class-specific system (12 skills total).
implementation: 1. gameStore: add mana/maxMana (class-specific scaling). 2. src/data/skills.ts: 13 SkillDefs tagged with classId, levelRequired, manaCost, description. 3. Warrior: power_strike (L1, 12mp, 2×dmg), battle_cry (L5, 15mp, -35% dmg 2rds), iron_skin (L8, 0mp, +30 def 2rds, monster attacks), whirlwind (L12, 28mp, attack twice). 4. Rogue: backstab (L1, 10mp, 3× crit guaranteed), shadow_step (L5, 10mp, monster cannot retaliate), rapid_strike (L8, 14mp, 3×70% hits), smoke_bomb (L12, 0mp, enemy hit -50% 2rds). 5. Sorcerer: fireball (L0, 28mp, 3× spellPower fire), meditate (L0, 0mp, 15HP+12mana/turn 3 turns, monster attacks), ice_blast (L6, 20mp, 2.5× spellPower cold, halves enemy hit), chain_lightning (L10, 25mp, 2× spellPower lightning, pierces resistance), mana_shield (L14, 0mp, absorb 50% dmg as mana 2rds). 6. combatStore: attackAction/skillAction/handleVictory all use deterministic RNG: makeRng(seed ^ (floor * 0x9e3779b9) ^ (roundNumber * 0x6b429177) ^ offset) — seed from useGameStore.getState().seed. No Date.now() in combat. 7. POST_COMBAT_RECOVERY = 0.10; handleVictory emits le('✦ You catch your breath — +X HP', 'heal') after every victory. 8. refillPotions() clears consumables then adds 3 generateHpPotion() + 1 generateManaPotion() — was previously only clearing without re-adding. 9. CombatScreen skill section: fixed grid layout. classSkills divided into rows of 2 (Math.ceil(classSkills.length / 2) rows). Null slots rendered as transparent 'skillBtnEmpty' placeholders to preserve grid shape. Warrior/rogue = 2×2, sorcerer = 3×2 (with 1 empty). skillGlyph fontSize: 24px (up from 14px). 10. Potion buttons show 'none' sub-label (potionEmptyNote style) when count === 0 in addition to the existing opacity:0.35 disabled state. 11. LevelUpOverlay.tsx: new nextSkill?: SkillDef | null prop. When newSkill is null but nextSkill is set, renders a dimmed 'NEXT SKILL' card showing name, description, and 'unlocks at level N'. CombatScreen tracks two states: levelUpSkill (unlocked now) and levelUpNextSkill (SKILLS.filter(classId, levelRequired > level).sort().at(0)). HUD mana bar in blue.

decisions.decision: Skill system uses a union SkillId type for compile-time exhaustiveness
decisions.rationale: TypeScript exhaustiveness ensures every SkillId is handled in switch statements; adding a new skill without handling it is a compile error.

decisions.decision: Skills defined in src/data/skills.ts as SkillDef objects with classId, levelRequired, manaCost, description
decisions.rationale: Centralised skill table makes balancing and adding skills straightforward; no logic lives in the data file.

decisions.decision: Skill execution dispatched through src/engine/skills/*.ts per class
decisions.rationale: Keeps combat engine logic separate from data definitions; each class file is independently testable.

decisions.decision: StatusCounters tracks per-round buff durations and cooldowns in combatStore
decisions.rationale: Buff durations and cooldowns are round-scoped state — owned by combatStore alongside the round counter so they tick in sync.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: spark added as sorcerer L0 offensive — 1x lightning spell power, 8 mana, fills 3x2 grid
decisions.rationale: Sorcerer had 5 skills leaving a null placeholder in the grid. Spark fills it and gives sorcerer an offensive starter that makes the opening floor viable before fireball.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: iron_skin and smoke_bomb given cooldownRounds:3 — prevents back-to-back defensive cycling
decisions.rationale: Without cooldowns, a player could loop iron_skin/smoke_bomb every round indefinitely, removing all risk from combat.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: whirlwind mana cost reduced 28 to 18
decisions.rationale: 28 mana blocked early warrior use of their signature skill — 18 makes it available earlier without being free.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: meditate reworked to mana-only — initial +20 on cast plus 20/round for 2 rounds = 60 total, no HP heal
decisions.rationale: Meditate was doing both HP and mana, making it a dominant skill. Nerfed from 30/round to 20/round to create real mana pressure for sorcerer and prevent 0% starve rate.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S4: Spark upgraded to 1.5× spell power + 0–6 variance, manaCost 8→6
decisions.rationale: Sim showed spark unviable vs basic attack at floor 1 when 1× mult. 1.5× makes it consistently better than physical (ignores defense) at all depths while 6-point variance tightens the damage range.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S5: power_strike levelRequired 1→0, backstab levelRequired 1→0
decisions.rationale: 3k-run sim showed level-0 characters had zero skills — basic attacks only — causing 100% mana starvation (no mana spent = never starved, but also no skill identity). Starting skills give each class an identity from the first combat.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S6: whirlwind levelRequired 12→8, manaCost 18→14
decisions.rationale: L12 locked warrior's signature AoE skill too late — players would clear Normal without ever using it. L8 lets warriors use it in mid-run before the boss, making it feel like a real toolkit option. Cost 14 matches rapid_strike for cross-class cost parity.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S6: smoke_bomb levelRequired 12→8, cooldownRounds 3→2, description -50%→-55% hit
decisions.rationale: Same issue as whirlwind — L12 made it unavailable in Normal difficulty. L8 gives rogue the defensive tool sooner. cd:2 (was 3) makes it more usable without being permanent uptime. -55% (was -50%) makes it clearly impactful on enemies with high dex/hit chance.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S6: spark manaCost 6→10
decisions.rationale: At 6mp, spark was too cheap relative to other skills — it was always the correct choice with no mana tension. 10mp creates a real decision between spark (ignores defense) and basic attack when mana is low.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S6: mana_shield levelRequired 14→10, absorption 50%→72%, duration 2→3 rounds
decisions.rationale: L14 was unreachable in Normal difficulty (typical clear level 8-10). L10 makes Mana Shield available for the Normal endgame. 72%/3rnd (was 50%/2rnd) makes it viable in Hell difficulty's long attrition fights — simulated 800-player diffTransitionSim validated that sorcerer Hell A5 reaches 24% (target 20%+) with this buff.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S5: power_strike 12→8mp, backstab 12→8mp, battle_cry 15→12mp
decisions.rationale: Sim showed warrior/rogue at 79%/71% mana starvation — too high. Reducing primary skill cost to 8 brings starvation to ~15%/8% (healthy tension range per fullRunSim).

decisions.decision: Balance pass S5: warrior baseMana 20→50, manaPerLevel 4→5; rogue baseMana 35→60
decisions.rationale: Old pools (warrior 20, rogue 35) forced classes into basic-attack-only play after 2-3 rounds. New pools give 6+ skill casts before running dry, with potions covering the rest.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S5: sorcerer spellPowerPerFloor 8→0, spellPowerPerLevel 1→7, baseSpellPower 10 added
decisions.rationale: Floor scaling made a sorcerer powerful just by going deep, regardless of XP earned. Level scaling ties spell power to character progression. Base 10 ensures sorcerer is functional at level 0.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S5: shadow_step reworked — no longer sets skipMonsterAttack, now gives 1.5× damage and 55% damage reduction (capped at 80%)
decisions.rationale: skipMonsterAttack made shadow_step a 'free damage' round with zero risk — OP in all scenarios. Damage reduction trades offense for survivability and stacks with battle_cry, creating cross-class combos.

decisions.decision: Balance pass S5: getBaseItemsForFloor excludes gem and rune slots
decisions.rationale: Gems and runes were in BASE_ITEMS and were being generated as random item drops via generateItem, then ALSO dropping via explicit gem/runeChance rolls. Double-counting inflated rune/gem counts 3-5×.

decisions.decision: fullRunSim.ts: comprehensive 3000-run multi-class balance harness
decisions.rationale: classBalanceSim only modelled a single floor. fullRunSim tracks gear accumulation, mana potions, skill cooldowns, shrine healing, XP/level progression, and loot over 10 floors — exposing balance issues invisible to the per-floor sim.

successCriteria: Each class sees only its own skills in CombatScreen. Power Strike deals 2× damage; Backstab always crits at 3× multiplier; Fireball deals fire damage scaling with spellPower. Meditate restores HP+mana while monster attacks. Iron Skin and Smoke Bomb are 0-cost defensive skills. Skill buttons greyed when mana insufficient or level below requirement. HUD mana bar updates in real time. Mana persists across floors. Level-up overlay shows next upcoming skill (name, level, description) when no skill unlocks at current level. Skill grid is fixed 2×2 (warrior/rogue) or 2×3 (sorcerer) with transparent locked-skill placeholders maintaining grid shape. Skill glyph is 24px. Potion buttons display 'none' sub-label when count is 0. Post-combat recovery is 10% with a '✦ You catch your breath' heal log line. Combat RNG is deterministic: identical seed/floor/round produces identical outcomes. refillPotions correctly adds 3 HP potions + 1 mana potion after clearing.
knownLimitations: Shadow Step→Backstab combo is log-only — no actual damage amplification implemented yet
Cooldown UI shows CD:N in skill meta but no countdown animation
Skill unlock requires level check but no UI indicator of what unlocks at what level
recommendations: Add skills section to CodexScreen showing each skill, level requirement, mana cost, and effect. Consider mana potions as a future rare item type. Battle Cry visual indicator (a glowing border around player HP?) would improve readability.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: BMAD session 2 improvements: fixed skill grid (2×2/2×3), 24px glyph, next-skill level-up preview, deterministic combat RNG, 10% post-combat recovery with log line, refillPotions fix

revisions.author: claude+marc
revisions.reason: Balance pass S4 — Spark spellVarianceFlat:12 (8–20 floor-1 range), Whirlwind guaranteed 2nd hit, Rapid Strike bonusHitChance:30 (30% 4th-hit proc). 205 balance tests green.

revisions.author: claude+marc
revisions.reason: Balance pass S6: whirlwind L12→L8/18→14mp; smoke_bomb L12→L8/cd:3→cd:2/-50%→-55%; spark 6→10mp; mana_shield L14→L10/50%→72%/2→3rnd. All 3 classes BALANCED per 800-player diffTransitionSim (NM A1: 19-31%, NM A5: 64-78%, Hell A1: 9-25%, Hell A5: 24-50%)

annotations.author: lac advance
annotations.body: BMAD session 3: added spark skill (sorcerer L0), iron_skin/smoke_bomb 3-round cooldowns, whirlwind mana 28→18, meditate reworked to mana-only (30+30+30=90), shadow_step→backstab combo log

publicInterface.name: SKILLS
publicInterface.description: Array of 13 skill definitions, each tagged with classId. Use getSkillsForClass(classId) to get a player's available skills.

publicInterface.name: SkillDef
publicInterface.description: { id, name, classId, levelRequired, manaCost, description }

publicInterface.name: getSkillsForClass
publicInterface.description: Returns only the skills for the given class, in unlock order

publicInterface.name: mana / maxMana
publicInterface.description: Current and max mana pool, persists across floors, shown in HUD. maxMana = classDef.baseMana + level × classDef.manaPerLevel.

codeSnippets.label: Sorcerer spell power formula (level-scaled, not floor-scaled)
Mana pool formulas (class baseMana + perLevel scaling + late-game passives)
getBaseItemsForFloor — excludes gems/runes
codeSnippets.snippet: return BASE_ITEMS.filter(i => i.minFloor <= floor && i.slot !== 'gem' && i.slot !== 'rune')

userGuide:
Skills are class-specific and unlock as you level up. Only your class's skills appear in combat — arranged in a fixed grid so you can always see what is locked and what is coming.

**Warrior (2×2 grid):** Power Strike (L0, 8mp) — doubles your damage from the very first combat. Battle Cry (L5, 12mp) — take 35% less damage for 2 rounds. Iron Skin (L8, free, cd:3) — +30 defense for 2 rounds, monster still attacks this round. Whirlwind (L8, 14mp) — attack twice, second hit always connects.

**Rogue (2×2 grid):** Backstab (L0, 8mp) — guaranteed crit at 2× (removes all crit RNG). Shadow Step (L5, 14mp) — strike first for 1.5× damage, monster retaliates at only 45% power. Rapid Strike (L8, 14mp) — 3 hits at 60% damage, 25% chance to trigger a 4th hit. Smoke Bomb (L8, free, cd:2) — enemy hit chance -55% for 2 rounds, they still attack this round.

**Sorcerer (3×2 grid):** Spark (L0, 10mp) — 1.5× spell power as lightning, ignores defense. Viable from floor 1, your most mana-efficient damage skill. Fireball (L4, 28mp) — 3× spell power fire, big burst but mana-heavy. Meditate (L0, free) — skip attack, regen 20 mana per turn for 3 turns (60 total). Ice Blast (L6, 20mp) — 2.5× spell power cold, halves enemy hit chance same round. Chain Lightning (L10, 25mp) — 2× spell power lightning, fully pierces resistance. Mana Shield (L10, free) — absorb 72% of incoming damage as mana for 3 rounds.

Sorcerer spell power = 10 (base) + 7×level. Mana Shield at 72%/3rnd is especially valuable in Hell difficulty's long attrition fights. All three classes have identical mana pools at L0 (warrior 50, rogue/sorc 60) — warrior gets more from base pool, sorc from passive regeneration.

Mana potions drop frequently from monsters. After each combat victory you automatically recover some HP ('✦ You catch your breath').

codexEntry.title: Active Skills
codexEntry.summary: Each class has 4–6 unique skills. Skills replace your normal attack and cost mana (some are free but have cooldowns or trade-offs).
mechanics: WARRIOR — Power Strike (L0, 8mp): 2× dmg | Battle Cry (L5, 12mp): -35% dmg taken 2rds | Iron Skin (L8, free, cd:3): +30 def 2rds, monster attacks | Whirlwind (L8, 14mp): attack twice — 2nd hit always connects

ROGUE — Backstab (L0, 8mp): guaranteed 2× crit (no RNG) | Shadow Step (L5, 14mp): 1.5× dmg, monster retaliates at 45% | Rapid Strike (L8, 14mp): 3×60% hits + 25% 4th hit | Smoke Bomb (L8, free, cd:2): enemy hit -55% for 2rds

SORCERER — Spark (L0, 10mp): 1.5×SP lightning, ignores defense | Fireball (L4, 28mp): 3×SP fire | Meditate (L0, free): skip attack, +20 mana/turn for 3 turns (60 total) | Ice Blast (L6, 20mp): 2.5×SP cold, halves enemy hit | Chain Lightning (L10, 25mp): 2×SP lightning, pierces resist | Mana Shield (L10, free): 72% dmg→mana for 3 rounds
SP = spell power = 10 + 7×level (Sorcerer only, level-tied not floor-tied). Warrior and Rogue have SP=0.
Using a skill replaces your normal attack — one action per round
Mana persists across floors; potions and meditate restore mana between/during fights
Greyed-out skills mean: insufficient mana OR level requirement not met
Cooldown skills (Iron Skin cd:3, Smoke Bomb cd:2) show 'CD:N' until usable again
tips: Warrior: Iron Skin is free with cd:3 — open with it on tough enemies. Whirlwind at L8 with 14mp is your highest DPS skill (two hits guaranteed).
Rogue: Backstab removes all crit RNG — it's exactly 2× damage, always. Smoke Bomb at L8 (cd:2) halves enemy accuracy for 2 rounds, making it very strong on bosses.
Sorcerer: Mana Shield (L10, 72%/3rnd) is the key to surviving Hell difficulty's long attrition fights. Cast it when a big hit is incoming — it converts the damage to mana, keeping you alive longer.
Sorcerer mana efficiency: Spark (10mp, 1.5×SP ignoring defense) is usually better than Fireball (28mp, 3×SP) when SP is low or the enemy has low HP. Save Fireball for high-HP elite/boss fights.
All cooldown skills tick down every round regardless of what action you take — you don't need to stay in combat to reset them.

statusHistory.reason: BMAD session 3: added spark skill (sorcerer L0), iron_skin/smoke_bomb 3-round cooldowns, whirlwind mana 28→18, meditate reworked to mana-only (30+30+30=90), shadow_step→backstab combo log

implementationNotes: fullRunSim.ts (3000 runs/class) is the canonical balance harness — any future skill cost or damage changes should be validated by running it before shipping.

Mana pools were originally sized for a floor-by-floor model with refillPotions(); once persistent mana was added, the original warrior:20/rogue:35 pools starved players within 2-3 rounds. The new pools (warrior:50, rogue:60) are calibrated against fullRunSim's 15%/8% starvation target.
baseSpellPower on ClassDef is a new field added so sorcerer is functional at level 0 after moving all scaling to spellPowerPerLevel. Without it, spark does 0 damage on turn 1.
getBaseItemsForFloor now excludes gem and rune slots — these had dedicated rollLoot drop mechanics, and including them in the random item pool caused 3-5x inflation of gem/rune counts.

shadow_step's skipMonsterAttack was the most overpowered skill — a free damage round. The replacement (1.5x damage + 55% dmg reduction) keeps it strong but gives the monster a role and stacks with battle_cry.
